commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Contributor proof and maintainer acceptance of the provider-bound structured serialization boundary are blockers; there is no narrow code repair identified in this read-only pass.; Needs attention: The diff has no dependency, workflow, lockfile, install-script, or code-execution change, but it expands provider-bound model context to include serialized structured tool-result blocks. (sha=a84cb9771826ed182fde25effcf4518e94e88049)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a shared structured tool-result text extractor and routes OpenAI Responses, OpenAI-compatible completions, Google/Gemini conversion, and the OpenAI Responses transport replay path through it with regression tests.

PR surface: Source +109, Tests +268. Total +377 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source for the structured-block slice: current main filters affected provider and transport replay down to explicit text blocks, so structured non-image blocks become empty text and hit existing fallback handling. The broader live issue still needs current-head proof because later comments tie the symptom to truncation, failover, and per-session degradation.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor proof and maintainer acceptance of the provider-bound structured serialization boundary are blockers; there is no narrow code repair identified in this read-only pass.

target.title: fix(llm): preserve structured tool result text across providers
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where agent sessions could receive literal `(see attached image)` placeholders instead of normal text/JSON tool results when a tool result block was structured but not typed as `text`.

This replacement PR covers the provider conversion paths from #96895 and also covers the active OpenAI Responses transport path noted in the review there.

Closes #96857
Related: #96895

## Why This Change Was Made

Tool-result replay code in multiple provider/transport paths only collected blocks with `type: "text"`. Structured non-image blocks could therefore be treated as empty output and collapse to an image/no-output fallback. This change adds a bounded shared extractor for structured non-image tool-result blocks and uses it in OpenAI Responses, OpenAI-compatible chat completions, Google/Gemini conversion, and the OpenAI Responses transport converter.

The current `main` fallback behavior is preserved:

- empty non-image output remains `(no output)` where that fallback already exists;
- image-only output remains `(see attached image)`;
- structured non-image output is serialized as text instead of being dropped.

## User Impact

Agents should be able to inspect structured text-like tool output in their model context instead of losing it to an attachment placeholder. This directly improves debugging and agent follow-up reliability when tools return JSON/status-like blocks.

## Evidence

- Live issue evidence: #96857 includes current redacted observations where ordinary text/status tool outputs reached an agent-visible context as literal `(see attached image)` placeholders instead of text.
- Code-path evidence: current `main` filtered tool-result content down to `type: "text"` in the affected provider/transport paths; this patch routes those paths through one bounded structured-text extractor.
- Local pre-check only:
  - `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` completed without whitespace errors.
  - The repository pre-commit formatter hook ran `oxfmt --write` on the 10 changed files before commit.
- GitHub Actions validation: pending on this PR. The PR is draft until the relevant CI/check lanes and real behavior proof are available for the current head SHA.

## Risk

- Message-delivery behavior changes are intentionally limited to structured non-image tool-result blocks that were previously dropped by text-only filtering.
- Image blocks remain excluded from text extraction, so image-bearing tool outputs keep using the existing image handling path.
- Structured blocks are serialized with bounded length and circular-reference handling to avoid unbounded replay text.

## Rollback

Revert this PR to restore the previous per-provider text-only extraction behavior. No migration, dependency, workflow, or persistent data changes are included.

## Not Tested / Limitations

- Full CI, typecheck, focused Vitest, and real behavior proof are not complete yet for this replacement PR head.
- This PR is intentionally draft until those GitHub Actions results are available and the body can be updated with current-head run links/results.
target.author: snowzlmbot

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
